title: Response Commitment, Problem Details, Streaming, and Downloads · Stakeholder TypeScript Mission
summary: A regional public-status and document operations team. They need defended middleware, runtime validation, trusted authority, cache-safe representations, bounded bodies and uploads, useful problem responses, and cancellation-safe streaming. Planned artifact: Deliver Validated and Bounded Streaming API with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Separate transport parsing, domain decisions, persistence, external effects, and serialization while assigning exactly one response owner.
Map domain results through explicit response schemas that minimize fields, stabilize nullability, and detect accidental secret or internal-property disclosure.
Record bounded route, request ID, trusted peer, and redacted target evidence without logging credentials, tokens, personal data, or unbounded query values.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Project Transport Domain Response Owner
steps.instruction:
project case — A project team handles case 9892 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose one response shape and stop a producer at the stream backpressure boundary; preserve response ownership after a client abort; then reuse an idempotency key with different input and reject the fingerprint mismatch. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate transport parsing, domain decisions, persistence, external effects, and serialization while assigning exactly one response owner.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Project Transport Domain Response Owner.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate transport parsing, domain decisions, persistence, external effects, and serialization while assigning exactly one response owner. For Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Project Transport Domain Response Owner, keep Transport Domain Response Owner connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

Competency probe: Use stream pipeline, write backpressure, bounded buffers, cancellation, error ownership, trailers, and finish-versus-close evidence for large responses.

For Node and Express server Test an acceptance boundary · Project Stream Backpressure Pipeline, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Calling response.write repeatedly cannot increase memory because Node streams always pause the producer automatically.

Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.
